title: VCD generation broken
body:
I see the issue with the [cva6](https://github.com/openhwgroup/cva6) design. the design builds fine without VCD generation (`make verilate`) but it fails with VCD generation (`make verilate DEBUG=1`):

````
Variane_testharness__Trace__2__Slow.cpp: In static member function ‘static void Variane_testharness::traceInitSub9(void*, VerilatedVcd*, const char*)’:
Variane_testharness__Trace__2__Slow.cpp:3354:71: error: invalid conversion from ‘const char*’ to ‘int’ [-fpermissive]
 3354 |         tracep->declBus(c+24455,Verilated::catName(scopep,"BAUD_RATE"," "), false,-1, 31,0);
      |                                                                       ^~~
      |                                                                       |
      |                                                                       const char*
In file included from /usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated_vcd_c.h:24,
                 from Variane_testharness__Trace__2__Slow.cpp:3:
/usr/local/share/verilator/include/verilated.h:844:68: note:   initializing argument 3 of ‘static const char* Verilated::catName(const char*, const char*, int, const char*)’
  844 |     static const char* catName(const char* n1, const char* n2, int scopet = 0,
      |                                                                ~~~~^~~~~~~~~~

````

My understanding is that verilator generate code that does not compile against its current code base. I spotted the following code which does not generate the "scopet" argument:

https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/c69ddc46f87ac3424fe3770e6fec5c751cf972a5/src/V3EmitCSyms.cpp#L711-L714
